title: Nintendo DS
content:
Iwata Asks HomeNintendo DS
1. Making a Completely New Sequel for the Nintendo DS system2. A Brand New Pokﾃδｩmon World3. Connecting for More Fun4. The Unchanging Qualities of Pokﾃδｩmon5. New Encounters in a New World
Making a Completely New Sequel for the Nintendo DS systemIwataToday we are at The Pokﾃδｩmon Company.1 I would like to "ask" about the Pokﾃδｩmon Black and Pokﾃδｩmon White Version games, which are just about to be released. Producer Ishihara-san is here, as well as Masuda-san and Sugimori-san from Game Freak2, the main developers of the game. Thank you for coming.EveryoneIt's our pleasure.
1. The Pokﾃδｩmon Company: In addition to brand management of everything related to Pokﾃδｩmon, including video games and the card game, the company operates six Pokﾃδｩmon Centers around Japan. Founded in the year 2000.
2. GAME FREAK, Inc.: The video game developer responsible for the Pokﾃδｩmon series and other titles. Founded in 1989.
IwataFirst, I would like to ask how you began talking about making a second completely new pair of Pokﾃδｩmon games for Nintendo DS. I suppose I should ask the director about this.MasudaYes. I had always wanted to make a second generation for Nintendo DS. Because the Nintendo DS family of systems has spread around the world to such an amazing degree.IwataThere are more than 100 million out there.MasudaRight. If possible, I wanted everyone to own at least one, and began development with that huge ambition.IwataJust like the last pair, Pokﾃδｩmon Black and Pokﾃδｩmon White Version games will be coming out for Nintendo DS, but your basic stance when you began this time was quite different than it was for the Pokﾃδｩmon Diamond and Pearl Versions games3, wasn't it?
3. Pokﾃδｩmon Diamond Version and Pokﾃδｩmon Pearl Version: First released for the Nintendo DS邃｢ system in Japan on September 28, 2006.
MasudaYes. Since this generation would be presented on the same system, I was extremely worried that it would turn out to be basically the same as its predecessor. If we tried to make it the same way as normal, I thought the games would turn out to be similar to Pokﾃδｩmon Diamond and Pokﾃδｩmon Pearl Versions.IwataBecause you're so used to making them.MasudaRight. So we started by changing our basic approach.IwataYou began with the question of how you couldﾃ｢ﾂﾂ杷or lack of a better way of putting itﾃ｢ﾂﾂ巴reak down everyone's image of what Pokﾃδｩmon games should be like on Nintendo DS.MasudaYeah. Working on the series for so long, our mindset had become fixed. Trading Pokﾃδｩmon by going to a Pokﾃδｩmon Center had become a matter of course. We needed to break down those fixed conceptions at first.IwataYou decided to use this opportunity to rethink certain things that had built up over the long history of the Pokﾃδｩmon games, such as how you make them and the rules that govern them.MasudaExactly. Also, I'd always made the Pokﾃδｩmon games with a desire to see everyone from children to adults play them, but it wasn't uncommon for players to move from junior high to high school to college and then feel as if they had graduated from Pokﾃδｩmon. I really found that to be regrettable.So I thought long and hard about what would keep me playing year after year. I reexamined the games from a variety of angles, like "Would I play a little longer if cutting-edge elements were added?" or "What about using Japanese kanji characters instead of hiragana?"IwataThis time, instead of using just hiragana, you can select kanji mode*.
(*Editor's note: In the Japanese version of the games, players can choose either the kanji or hiragana modes which changes the types of the characters in which the dialogue is displayed depending on their reading skills. The English version has only has one reading mode.)
MasudaI thought some people would find kanji easier to read.Iwata
So after the Pokﾃδｩmon Diamond and Pokﾃδｩmon Pearl Version games came out and everyone at Game Freak was making Pokﾃδｩmon Platinum Version4, you were examining the games from a number of different angles and thinking about how you could make them so that players never "graduated" from them.
4. Pokﾃδｩmon Platinum Version: First released in Japan for the Nintendo DS system on September 13, 2008 as the new version of Pokﾃδｩmon Diamond Version and Pokﾃδｩmon Pearl Version.
MasudaYes, I kept thinking about it for roughly two years. But when we were making the Pokﾃδｩmon Diamond and Pokﾃδｩmon Pearl Version games, we didn't have to think too hard in order to establish a difference with previous games, because we had the DS as new hardware.IwataSimply making the games for the Nintendo DS created a distinction.MasudaCompared to the Game Boy Advance system, the wireless functionality had improved dramatically, online connectivity had been added, and it could be operated by touch. But if we had approached Pokﾃδｩmon Black and Pokﾃδｩmon White Versions with the same attitude as the previous games, they would have turned out the same, so this time a new mentality was absolutely crucial.IwataI see. As the producer, Ishihara-san, it was your role to listen to Masuda-san's ideas about what he wanted to do before anyone else.IshiharaYes, that's right.IwataIn general, producers tend to adopt the view that if something is being received well, you shouldn't mess with it too much, so when Masuda-san told you he wanted to fundamentally rethink the Pokﾃδｩmon games, was your happiness at his desire to make big changes mingled with nervous excitement over the same?Ishihara
My happiness was overwhelmingly the stronger of the two. The Pokﾃδｩmon games began with Pokﾃδｩmon Red and Pokﾃδｩmon Green Versions5 (Pokﾃδｩmon Red and Pokﾃδｩmon Blue Versions in the US) for the Game Boy system, and then Pokﾃδｩmon Gold and Pokﾃδｩmon Silver Versions6 came out for Game Boy Colorﾃ｢ﾂﾂｦ Iwata Game Boy Advance had Pokﾃδｩmon Ruby and Pokﾃδｩmon Sapphire Versions7, and the Nintendo DS has Pokﾃδｩmon Diamond and Pokﾃδｩmon Pearl Versions.
5. Pokﾃδｩmon Red and Pokﾃδｩmon Green Versions: The first generation of games in the Pokﾃδｩmon series. First released in Japan for the Game Boy邃｢ system on February 27, 1996.
6. Pokﾃδｩmon Gold and Pokﾃδｩmon Silver Versions: The second generation of games in the Pokﾃδｩmon series. The games were compatible with the Game Boy Color system, so Pokﾃδｩmon appeared in various colors. First released in Japan on November 21, 1999.
7. Pokﾃδｩmon Ruby and Pokﾃδｩmon Sapphire Versions: The third generation of games in the Pokﾃδｩmon series. First released in Japan for the Game Boy Advance system on November 21, 2002.
IshiharaIt has been the series' destiny to transfer to a completely new platform every time a completely new generation came out. The DS, however, has had a long lifespan and spread around the world. We were able to make a second generation for Nintendo DS by, from the point of view of development, making effective use of already-existing resources.
IwataYou sensed that you could use your know-how developing for Nintendo DSﾃ｢ﾂﾂ輩our "resources" cultivated in development of Pokﾃδｩmon Diamond and Pokﾃδｩmon Pearl Versionsﾃ｢ﾂﾂ琶n making Pokﾃδｩmon Black and Pokﾃδｩmon White Versions.IshiharaRight. There were a lot of merits this time to polishing up what we had figured out before, but like Masuda-san said earlier, when it comes to bringing games out for the same platform, unless you really put a lot of effort into creating something distinctly new and differentﾃ｢ﾂﾂｦIwataPeople will say, "Is this all that's different?!"IshiharaRight. You run the risk of people saying, "You call this a completely new game, butﾃ｢ﾂﾂｦ" We were really worried about that. But when I heard Masuda-san's first explanation of his thoughts, I thought, "You're gonna change that much?!" and "Can we really pull this off?"IwataThe changes were so big that even a grizzled veteran such as yourself wondered if it was possible?IshiharaThat's right.IwataSugimori-san, what was your first impression when you heard what Masuda-san wanted to do?Sugimori
Masuda gives us a sort of policy speech each time outlining what he wants to do.
IwataA policy speech? (laughs)SugimoriYeah. And usually a lot of it is fairly eyebrow-raising.IwataSo this time too, you were like, "Again?!" (laughs)SugimoriYeah. (laughs)MasudaBut this time all the Pokﾃδｩmon are new, so I bet you were especially surprised.SugimoriWell, at first when he said we were going to make all new Pokﾃδｩmon, I said things like, "Huh?! Ease up a little, would ya?!" and "Can't we cut down on them a little?" But a few grumbles aren't enough to get this guy to change the contents of his policy speech. (laughs)IwataHe's stubborn. (laughs)MasudaYeah. (laughs) When he said, "Can't we cut down on it a little?" I would look at him like this (looking into Sugimori-san's face) and say, "You can make it work somehow, can't you?"Sugimori(laughs)IshiharaI get the feeling Masahiro Sakurai-san8 doesn't budge either.IwataAh, that explains it to me quite succinctly. (laughs)Everyone(laughs)IshiharaHe shows us something and says, "This is what we're gonna make," and everyone's like, "Huh? We're gonna make that?!" You might think he'll change his mind somewhere along the way, but he never does.IwataHe runs with it all the way to the end.IshiharaYep. It's hard for everyone else to keep up. (laughs)MasudaI think the staff had quite a hard time of it this time.
8. Masahiro Sakurai: Director of games in the Kirby邃｢ and Super Smash Bros.邃｢ series. He is currently developing Kid Icarus邃｢: Uprising for the upcoming Nintendo 3DS邃｢ system.
